answers:
### 1. Production config values that should not be there

`medislot/infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars` is a **byte-for-byte copy of `infra/staging/app.auto.tfvars`** (I diffed them by reading both; all six lines identical). Every developer-convenience / staging value was inherited. Setting by setting:

- **`ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` (prod/app.auto.tfvars:2)** — debug toolbar on in production. Per the do-not-inherit scrub, this should be **omitted entirely**, not set to `false`. `config.py:8` treats it as on only when `== "true"`, so removing the line is safe and correct.
- **`AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` (line 3)** — relaxed authentication in production. **Omit the line entirely** (not set false).
- **`PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` (line 4)** — a preset test account with superadmin privilege baked into prod. This is exactly the "allow-list of internal/test accounts with elevated privileges" the scrub says to remove. **Omit entirely.**
- **`SHADOW_SCORING_ENABLED = "false"` (line 5)** and **`SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"` (line 6)** — the experimental shadow-scoring feature. Setting `ENABLED=false` is **not sufficient**: `config.py:18-22` `record_shadow_observation` writes to the sink whenever `shadow_scoring_sink` is non-empty and *never consults* `shadow_scoring_enabled`. So the stray non-empty `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` value silently re-arms the feature — and it points at a **staging** S3 path. Both keys should be **fully absent** from prod config.
- **`LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"` (line 7)** — a secret injection from a previous billing architecture. The scrub says not to recreate a known dead fossil in the new environment. Unless prod genuinely still uses legacy billing (nothing in the code reads it beyond storing the string), **omit it**; recreating a shared/old secret ARN in prod is a needless credential.
- **`infra/prod/main.tf:3` — `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`** — even the prod Terraform still names `environment:staging`. This must change to `...:environment:production` (see answer 4). Only line 7's service name (`medislot-prod`) was actually edited during the copy; the trust condition was not.

The net-correct prod `app.auto.tfvars` would contain **none** of these six keys (or only a genuinely prod-scoped billing secret if legacy billing is truly still in use).

### 2. How the production frontend is built and where its API base URL comes from

- The React app reads its API base URL from `import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL` at build time (`frontend/src.txt`).
- `frontend/vite.config.ts` comments confirm "VITE_-prefixed variables are inlined into the built bundle at build time" — i.e., the URL is **baked into the static bundle**, not read at runtime.
- The value comes from a `.env.<mode>` file selected by Vite's `--mode`. The only such file present is `frontend/.env.staging`, which sets `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com`. **There is no `.env.production`** (confirmed by `ls frontend/.env*`).
- In `deploy.yml:14` the build step runs `npx vite build --mode staging`, so the bundle is built with `mode=staging` → it picks up `.env.staging` → the **staging API URL is inlined**.
- That single bundle is packaged into one image (`deploy.yml:16-21`) and the **same digest** is deployed to both staging (`deploy-staging`) and prod (`deploy-prod`, line 34: "deploy the same already-built digest to prod").

Consequence (the skill's "rebuild per environment" rule): the production frontend would ship a bundle hard-coded to call `https://staging-api.example.com`. There is no runtime override available. Prod needs its own `.env.production` with the prod API URL and a separate `vite build --mode production` (and ideally an output-scan check that the wrong environment's URL isn't present before shipping).

### 3. What the deploy pipeline does, and what's missing

`.github/workflows/deploy.yml` triggers on push to `main` and runs three jobs:
1. **build-frontend** — checkout, `npm ci`, `npx vite build --mode staging` (staging bundle, see #2), `docker build`/`docker push` of `registry.example.com/medislot:latest`, and outputs the image digest.
2. **deploy-staging** (`environment: staging`) — runs `./scripts/deploy.sh "<digest>" staging`.
3. **deploy-prod** (`needs: [build-frontend, deploy-staging]`, `environment: production`) — runs `./scripts/deploy.sh "<digest>" prod` with the *same* digest.

Missing / broken for this first prod release:
- **The deploy script does not exist.** `deploy.yml` calls `./scripts/deploy.sh`, but there is no `scripts/` directory anywhere in `medislot/` (confirmed by `ls scripts` → "No such file or directory" and `find`). Both deploy jobs will fail immediately. This is exactly the skill's "verify the ops/seed script actually exists in the deploy" trap.
- **Prod infra has never been applied.** `infra/prod/` has no `terraform.tfstate` (staging has one at serial 41). The prod `synthetic_app_service`/deploy role don't exist yet, so a pipeline deploy has nothing to deploy to until `terraform apply` is run for prod first.
- **Migrations are unproven.** Alembic migrations exist (`backend/migrations/versions/0001_base.py`, `0002_tenants.py`, `0003_billing.py`). The runbook (`docs/prod-runbook.md`, "last updated: 3 months ago") claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but that automation would live in the missing `deploy.sh`. With the script absent, nothing runs migrations. Per the skill's first-apply trap, don't assume the pipeline migrates the fresh prod DB — this needs explicit confirmation/first manual pass.
- **Wrong-environment build artifact** (see #2): even if the script existed, prod would get the staging-built bundle/digest.
- **First-apply traps not yet handled**: fresh prod DNS/cert validation may time out on first apply; the container registry image tag exists (`:latest` from staging builds) but prod compute/role must be created first; and any prod secrets seeded at infra-creation must be replaced with real values before first real traffic.

### 4. How CI authenticates for staging vs. production

Cloud auth is via GitHub OIDC assuming a deploy IAM role. The role is defined in `infra/modules/deploy_role/main.tf`: `synthetic_iam_role.deploy`, hard-coded name `"medislot-deploy"`, with `assume_role_condition["token.actions.example.com:sub"] = var.oidc_sub_condition`.

- **Staging** (`infra/staging/main.tf:3`) passes `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`. The `deploy-staging` job runs with `environment: staging` (`deploy.yml:25`), so its OIDC sub claim is `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging`, which matches. This role is real and applied (present in `infra/staging/terraform.tfstate`).
- **Production** (`infra/prod/main.tf:3`) currently passes the **same** `oidc_sub_condition = "...:environment:staging"` — the copy was never edited. But the `deploy-prod` job runs with `environment: production` (`deploy.yml:32`), so its OIDC sub claim would be `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`. That **does not match** the trust condition, so prod deploys would fail to authenticate even once the script/state exist. Additionally, the module hard-codes the role name `medislot-deploy` for both environments, so applying prod would collide with / not be scoped separately from the existing staging role.

Fix (skill's rule that a stricter environment needs its own scoped trust condition, never inherited/shared): set `infra/prod/main.tf` `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`, and give the prod role a distinct name (e.g., parameterize the module's role name) so prod has its own scoped, non-shared deploy identity.
report:
## Plan

I set out to review the unapplied production setup for MediSlot (`medislot/`) before its first launch (REL-140), following the staging-to-prod-cutover-campaign skill: probe real state rather than trusting docs, run the "do-not-inherit scrub" on config promoted from staging, check the frontend build-artifact-per-environment rule, walk the deploy pipeline for first-apply traps and missing pieces, and verify per-environment CI auth. I checked every file under `medislot/` and compared `infra/prod/` against the already-live `infra/staging/`.

## Actions

- Read `.claude/skills/staging-to-prod-cutover-campaign/SKILL.md` — gave me the do-not-inherit scrub checklist, first-apply traps, the "rebuild per environment / don't promote a build artifact" rule, the ops-script-exists check, and the "prod needs its own scoped deploy-role trust condition" rule. (Shaped the whole approach.)
- Listed the repo tree; the production project is `medislot/`.
- Read `medislot/infra/prod/main.tf`, `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars`, `infra/prod/README.md` — the prod config, confirmed "copied from staging… Not yet applied."
- Read `medislot/infra/staging/main.tf`, `infra/staging/app.auto.tfvars`, `infra/staging/terraform.tfstate` — the staging baseline; tfstate (serial 41) confirms staging is really applied, prod has no state file (never applied).
- Read `medislot/infra/modules/deploy_role/main.tf` — the shared deploy-role module; role name and trust condition.
- Read `medislot/.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — the pipeline.
- Read `medislot/backend/config.py` — how the env vars are consumed (notably `record_shadow_observation`).
- Read `medislot/frontend/.env.staging`, `frontend/vite.config.ts`, `frontend/src.txt` — frontend build/API-URL wiring.
- Read `medislot/docs/prod-runbook.md` (last updated 3 months ago) and `medislot/README.md`.
- Ran `ls scripts`, `ls frontend/.env*`, `find` over `medislot/` — confirmed no `scripts/deploy.sh` exists and no `.env.production` exists.

## Answers

### 1. Production config values that should not be there

`medislot/infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars` is a **byte-for-byte copy of `infra/staging/app.auto.tfvars`** (I diffed them by reading both; all six lines identical). Every developer-convenience / staging value was inherited. Setting by setting:

- **`ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` (prod/app.auto.tfvars:2)** — debug toolbar on in production. Per the do-not-inherit scrub, this should be **omitted entirely**, not set to `false`. `config.py:8` treats it as on only when `== "true"`, so removing the line is safe and correct.
- **`AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` (line 3)** — relaxed authentication in production. **Omit the line entirely** (not set false).
- **`PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` (line 4)** — a preset test account with superadmin privilege baked into prod. This is exactly the "allow-list of internal/test accounts with elevated privileges" the scrub says to remove. **Omit entirely.**
- **`SHADOW_SCORING_ENABLED = "false"` (line 5)** and **`SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"` (line 6)** — the experimental shadow-scoring feature. Setting `ENABLED=false` is **not sufficient**: `config.py:18-22` `record_shadow_observation` writes to the sink whenever `shadow_scoring_sink` is non-empty and *never consults* `shadow_scoring_enabled`. So the stray non-empty `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` value silently re-arms the feature — and it points at a **staging** S3 path. Both keys should be **fully absent** from prod config.
- **`LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"` (line 7)** — a secret injection from a previous billing architecture. The scrub says not to recreate a known dead fossil in the new environment. Unless prod genuinely still uses legacy billing (nothing in the code reads it beyond storing the string), **omit it**; recreating a shared/old secret ARN in prod is a needless credential.
- **`infra/prod/main.tf:3` — `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`** — even the prod Terraform still names `environment:staging`. This must change to `...:environment:production` (see answer 4). Only line 7's service name (`medislot-prod`) was actually edited during the copy; the trust condition was not.

The net-correct prod `app.auto.tfvars` would contain **none** of these six keys (or only a genuinely prod-scoped billing secret if legacy billing is truly still in use).

### 2. How the production frontend is built and where its API base URL comes from

- The React app reads its API base URL from `import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL` at build time (`frontend/src.txt`).
- `frontend/vite.config.ts` comments confirm "VITE_-prefixed variables are inlined into the built bundle at build time" — i.e., the URL is **baked into the static bundle**, not read at runtime.
- The value comes from a `.env.<mode>` file selected by Vite's `--mode`. The only such file present is `frontend/.env.staging`, which sets `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com`. **There is no `.env.production`** (confirmed by `ls frontend/.env*`).
- In `deploy.yml:14` the build step runs `npx vite build --mode staging`, so the bundle is built with `mode=staging` → it picks up `.env.staging` → the **staging API URL is inlined**.
- That single bundle is packaged into one image (`deploy.yml:16-21`) and the **same digest** is deployed to both staging (`deploy-staging`) and prod (`deploy-prod`, line 34: "deploy the same already-built digest to prod").

Consequence (the skill's "rebuild per environment" rule): the production frontend would ship a bundle hard-coded to call `https://staging-api.example.com`. There is no runtime override available. Prod needs its own `.env.production` with the prod API URL and a separate `vite build --mode production` (and ideally an output-scan check that the wrong environment's URL isn't present before shipping).

### 3. What the deploy pipeline does, and what's missing

`.github/workflows/deploy.yml` triggers on push to `main` and runs three jobs:
1. **build-frontend** — checkout, `npm ci`, `npx vite build --mode staging` (staging bundle, see #2), `docker build`/`docker push` of `registry.example.com/medislot:latest`, and outputs the image digest.
2. **deploy-staging** (`environment: staging`) — runs `./scripts/deploy.sh "<digest>" staging`.
3. **deploy-prod** (`needs: [build-frontend, deploy-staging]`, `environment: production`) — runs `./scripts/deploy.sh "<digest>" prod` with the *same* digest.

Missing / broken for this first prod release:
- **The deploy script does not exist.** `deploy.yml` calls `./scripts/deploy.sh`, but there is no `scripts/` directory anywhere in `medislot/` (confirmed by `ls scripts` → "No such file or directory" and `find`). Both deploy jobs will fail immediately. This is exactly the skill's "verify the ops/seed script actually exists in the deploy" trap.
- **Prod infra has never been applied.** `infra/prod/` has no `terraform.tfstate` (staging has one at serial 41). The prod `synthetic_app_service`/deploy role don't exist yet, so a pipeline deploy has nothing to deploy to until `terraform apply` is run for prod first.
- **Migrations are unproven.** Alembic migrations exist (`backend/migrations/versions/0001_base.py`, `0002_tenants.py`, `0003_billing.py`). The runbook (`docs/prod-runbook.md`, "last updated: 3 months ago") claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but that automation would live in the missing `deploy.sh`. With the script absent, nothing runs migrations. Per the skill's first-apply trap, don't assume the pipeline migrates the fresh prod DB — this needs explicit confirmation/first manual pass.
- **Wrong-environment build artifact** (see #2): even if the script existed, prod would get the staging-built bundle/digest.
- **First-apply traps not yet handled**: fresh prod DNS/cert validation may time out on first apply; the container registry image tag exists (`:latest` from staging builds) but prod compute/role must be created first; and any prod secrets seeded at infra-creation must be replaced with real values before first real traffic.

### 4. How CI authenticates for staging vs. production

Cloud auth is via GitHub OIDC assuming a deploy IAM role. The role is defined in `infra/modules/deploy_role/main.tf`: `synthetic_iam_role.deploy`, hard-coded name `"medislot-deploy"`, with `assume_role_condition["token.actions.example.com:sub"] = var.oidc_sub_condition`.

- **Staging** (`infra/staging/main.tf:3`) passes `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`. The `deploy-staging` job runs with `environment: staging` (`deploy.yml:25`), so its OIDC sub claim is `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging`, which matches. This role is real and applied (present in `infra/staging/terraform.tfstate`).
- **Production** (`infra/prod/main.tf:3`) currently passes the **same** `oidc_sub_condition = "...:environment:staging"` — the copy was never edited. But the `deploy-prod` job runs with `environment: production` (`deploy.yml:32`), so its OIDC sub claim would be `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`. That **does not match** the trust condition, so prod deploys would fail to authenticate even once the script/state exist. Additionally, the module hard-codes the role name `medislot-deploy` for both environments, so applying prod would collide with / not be scoped separately from the existing staging role.

Fix (skill's rule that a stricter environment needs its own scoped trust condition, never inherited/shared): set `infra/prod/main.tf` `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`, and give the prod role a distinct name (e.g., parameterize the module's role name) so prod has its own scoped, non-shared deploy identity.

## Next steps

If allowed to change things, I would: (1) rewrite `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars` to omit the six inherited keys (debug toolbar, relaxed auth, preset admin, both shadow-scoring keys, legacy billing secret); (2) set the prod `oidc_sub_condition` to `environment:production` and parameterize the deploy-role name per environment; (3) add `frontend/.env.production` with the real prod API URL and make the pipeline build/deploy a separate prod bundle (`--mode production`) rather than promoting the staging digest, plus an output scan for `staging-api`; (4) add the missing `scripts/deploy.sh` (and confirm/handle migrations explicitly for the first prod DB); (5) `terraform plan` prod (never apply without explicit go-ahead) to validate; and (6) treat prod first-apply DNS/cert and secret-seeding as expected manual steps. I would not merge/apply/push anything without an explicit in-the-moment go-ahead, since merging arms the auto-deploy pipeline.

## Confidence and gaps

- **High confidence** the prod tfvars is an unscrubbed copy (read both files; identical), that `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` re-arms shadow scoring regardless of the `false` flag (`config.py:18-22`), that the frontend bakes in the staging URL (`--mode staging` + only `.env.staging` exists), that `scripts/deploy.sh` is absent, that prod has never been applied (no tfstate), and that the prod OIDC condition still says `staging`.
- **Gaps / could not verify:** whether `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN` is truly a dead fossil vs. still needed in prod (no code reads it beyond storing the string, but I can't see the full billing implementation); whether migrations are wired elsewhere, since the referenced `deploy.sh` doesn't exist to inspect; and the actual prod API hostname (no prod env file exists to state it). I did not run Terraform, the pipeline, or any build, so those are static-analysis conclusions, not observed runs.

That **does not match** the trust condition, so prod deploys would fail to authenticate even once the script/state exist. Additionally, the module hard-codes the role name `medislot-deploy` for both environments, so applying prod would collide with / not be scoped separately from the existing staging role.

Fix (skill's rule that a stricter environment needs its own scoped trust condition, never inherited/shared): set `infra/prod/main.tf` `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`, and give the prod role a distinct name (e.g., parameterize the module's role name) so prod has its own scoped, non-shared deploy identity.
sections.confidence and gaps:
- **High confidence** the prod tfvars is an unscrubbed copy (read both files; identical), that `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` re-arms shadow scoring regardless of the `false` flag (`config.py:18-22`), that the frontend bakes in the staging URL (`--mode staging` + only `.env.staging` exists), that `scripts/deploy.sh` is absent, that prod has never been applied (no tfstate), and that the prod OIDC condition still says `staging`.
- **Gaps / could not verify:** whether `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN` is truly a dead fossil vs. still needed in prod (no code reads it beyond storing the string, but I can't see the full billing implementation); whether migrations are wired elsewhere, since the referenced `deploy.sh` doesn't exist to inspect; and the actual prod API hostname (no prod env file exists to state it). I did not run Terraform, the pipeline, or any build, so those are static-analysis conclusions, not observed runs.
sections.next steps: If allowed to change things, I would: (1) rewrite `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars` to omit the six inherited keys (debug toolbar, relaxed auth, preset admin, both shadow-scoring keys, legacy billing secret); (2) set the prod `oidc_sub_condition` to `environment:production` and parameterize the deploy-role name per environment; (3) add `frontend/.env.production` with the real prod API URL and make the pipeline build/deploy a separate prod bundle (`--mode production`) rather than promoting the staging digest, plus an output scan for `staging-api`; (4) add the missing `scripts/deploy.sh` (and confirm/handle migrations explicitly for the first prod DB); (5) `terraform plan` prod (never apply without explicit go-ahead) to validate; and (6) treat prod first-apply DNS/cert and secret-seeding as expected manual steps. I would not merge/apply/push anything without an explicit in-the-moment go-ahead, since merging arms the auto-deploy pipeline.
sections.plan: I set out to review the unapplied production setup for MediSlot (`medislot/`) before its first launch (REL-140), following the staging-to-prod-cutover-campaign skill: probe real state rather than trusting docs, run the "do-not-inherit scrub" on config promoted from staging, check the frontend build-artifact-per-environment rule, walk the deploy pipeline for first-apply traps and missing pieces, and verify per-environment CI auth. I checked every file under `medislot/` and compared `infra/prod/` against the already-live `infra/staging/`.
